data.title: Loar launches equity offering
data.description:
• Loar Holdings (NYSE:LOAR) has announced public offering of 4.75M common shares, including 1.58M shares offered by certain stockholders and rest by the company.
  • The underwriters have a 30-day option to purchase up to 712,500 additional shares of common stock at the public offering price.
  • The net proceeds will be used for repayment of borrowings outstanding under its credit agreement and, to the extent of any remaining proceeds, for general corporate purposes, including working capital.
  • LOAR -0.7% premarket to $91.3
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Renault is seen standing out amid tough backdrop for European auto stocks
data.description:
Investors are cautious about the European automobile sector due to significant uncertainties in 2025. Key unknowns identified recently by HSBC include potential regulatory relief or CO2 fines, possible U.S. import tariffs, the impact of local US production, and tough EU-China negotiations on electric vehicles tariffs. However, the firm also thinks that despite those challenges, share prices may already reflect the anticipated headwinds.

One reason for slight optimism on the European auto sector is that experts anticipate the EU will likely offer a phased approach to CO2 regulation, as imposing large fines on automakers could negatively impact job creation and electric vehicle affordability. European automakers are also expected to focus heavily on cost-cutting measures to improve profitability, given limited prospects for revenue growth.

HSBC views Renault SA (OTCPK:RNSDF) (OTCPK:RNLSY) favorably due to its limited exposure to China and the U.S. markets. BMW (OTCPK:BMWKY) (OTCPK:BAMXF), Mercedes-Benz group AG (OTCPK:MBGAF), and Porsche (OTCPK:POAHY) are also still considered attractive investments, but were highlighted as needing to demonstrate effective cost management strategies. Meanwhile, the firm thinks Volkswagen (OTCPK:VWAGY) (OTCPK:VLKAF) could surprise positively in 2025, given low market expectations and ongoing self-improvement efforts.

HSBC is more cautious on Stellantis (STLA), with the auto giant seen needing to prove its issues are temporary rather than structural.

Overall, the European auto market is forecast to grow 4.0% to 4.5% in 2025, despite the various challenges. The outlook for electric vehicle market share has worsened, with S&P Global revising its forecast for BEV market share down to 21% for 2025 from 27%.

data.title: Interpublic stock gains on deal to be acquired by peer Omnicom
data.description:
Omnicom (NYSE:OMC) to acquire Interpublic (NYSE:IPG) in a stock-for-stock transaction, sending the peer advertising company's shares rallying +15% in premarket trade.

Under the terms of the agreement, Interpublic (IPG) shareholders will receive 0.344 Omnicom (OMC) shares for each share of Interpublic common stock they own.

Following the close of the transaction, Omnicom (OMC) shareholders will own 60.6% of the combined company and Interpublic shareholders will own 39.4%, on a fully diluted basis. The transaction is expected to generate annual cost synergies of $750 million.

"This combination represents a tremendous strategic opportunity for our stakeholders, amplifying our investments in platform capabilities and talent as part of a more expansive network," said Philippe Krakowsky, Interpublic's CEO said.

John Wren will remain Chairman & CEO of Omnicom. Phil Angelastro will remain EVP & CFO of Omnicom.

Philippe Krakowsky and Daryl Simm will serve as Co-Presidents and COOs of Omnicom, while Krakowsky will also be Co-Chair of the Integration Committee post-merger.

The combined company will retain the Omnicom name and trade under the OMC ticker symbol on the New York Stock Exchange.

IPG +14.80% premarket to $33.59, Omnicom (OMC) down -1.54% to $101.83.

The all-stock agreement is likely to value Interpublic (IPG) in a range of $13 billion to $14 billion excluding debt, the Journal reported. Interpublic’s (IPG) market value was about $11 billion as of Friday.

Source: Press Release

data.title: AMD falls as BofA downgrades amid AI, PC concerns
data.description:
AMD (NASDAQ:AMD) was in focus on Monday as Bank of America downgraded the semiconductor company, citing lower estimates for artificial intelligence and PC-related revenue in 2025.

Shares fell 1.5% in premarket trading.

A higher competitive environment, specifically from Nvidia (NVDA), as well as the "growing" cloud preference for custom chips from Broadcom (AVGO) and Marvell Technology (MRVL) is likely to limit AMD's market share gain potential, Bank of America analyst Vivek Arya said in a note to clients.

Arya lowered his rating on AMD to Neutral from Buy and cut his 2025 and 2026 earnings estimates by 6% and 8%, respectively. He also lowered his price target to $155 from $180.

"While our forecast implies solid 54% YoY growth, the limited opportunity to exceed higher street estimates could continue to be an overhang on AMD stock," Arya wrote. "AMD’s pipeline remains 1yr+ behind NVDA’s (which is accelerating) and lacks a competitive networking (switching, optics) portfolio."

Arya also pointed to the recent comments from an Amazon (AMZN) executive that said the cloud service provider has "not yet" seen heavy demand for AMD's chips as a negative.

PC weakness

In addition, the PC market could correct in the first-half of 2025, which could bode negatively for the Dr. Lisa Su-led company.

"Even though the PC/server market are growing modestly, AMD still has a chance to expand its share from ~23% currently, given restructuring issues at leader INTC (69% share)," Arya added. "AMD’s challenge (and opportunity) in CY25 will be to take share in enterprise PC where INTC is dominant, while fending off threat from ARM-based (QCOM) rivals."

data.title: Apollo, Santander to invest in $370M infrastructure credit portfolio
data.description:
Apollo Global Management (NYSE:APO) and Banco Santander (NYSE:SAN) agreed to invest in an ~$370M portfolio of infrastructure credit, the companies said Monday.

The deal was led by Apterra, an Apollo affiliate that specializes in financing solutions for infrastructure projects.

"We have high conviction in the infrastructure finance opportunity globally given the large capital demands that will continue to drive investment in the sector and see continued opportunity to collaborate with Santander in the space," said Samuel Feinstein, Apollo (NYSE:APO) partner and president of ACT Capital.

Marcel Patino, global head of Private Debt Mobilization at Santander (NYSE:SAN) said,"We remain committed to private debt mobilization to generate additional capital for profitable growth as we continue to accelerate our business transformation efforts."

Apollo (APO) has deployed more than $40B into energy transition and climate-related investment and is actively seeking to increase its platform as capital deployment in these areas of the global economy continues to scale, the company said.

data.title: Universe Pharmaceuticals stock slides on capital raise of about $15M via stock offering
data.description:
• Universe Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:UPC) has entered into a definitive agreement with several investors to offer and sell 18.75 million ordinary shares at a purchase price of $0.80 a share or pre-funded warrant units priced at $0.799 each, for an expected gross proceeds to be approximately $15 million.
  • Additionally, the company consented to issue warrants to the same investors for the acquisition of up to 18.75 million common shares at an exercise price of $0.80 per share. The duration of the warrants will be five years from the date of issuance.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about December 10, 2024.
  • UPC is down 52.5% premarket to $1.61 on Monday.

data.title: Datadog to offer $775M convertible debt
data.description:
Datadog (NASDAQ:DDOG) plans to offer $775M aggregate principal amount of Convertible Senior Notes due 2029 in a private placement.

The company also intends to grant the initial purchasers of the notes an option to purchase, within a 13-day period beginning on, and including, the date on which the notes are first issued, up to an additional $116.25M of the notes.

The notes will accrue interest payable semiannually in arrears and will mature on December 1, 2029, unless earlier converted, redeemed or repurchased.

The interest rate, initial conversion rate, repurchase or redemption rights and other terms of the notes will be determined at the time of pricing of the offering.

Proceeds from the offering will be used to repurchase for cash a portion of Datadog's outstanding 0.125% Convertible Senior Notes due 2025, to pay the cost of the capped call transactions and for general corporate purposes.

DDOG shares dipped 1.30% premarket on Monday.

data.title: Six Flags lands a bull rating at Jefferies amid valuation that is 'easy on the eyes'
data.description:
Jefferies initiated coverage on Six Flags Entertainment Corporation (NYSE:FUN) on Monday with a Buy rating. The firm sees the combination of Six Flags and Cedar Fair presenting a beneficial long-term opportunity for investors, with Cedar Fair's management well positioned to maximize the true earnings potential of the combined theme part operator.

"We believe that the integration will be relatively smooth, as FUN's and SIX's parks have little geographic overlap. We also believe that the execution opportunities in technology, labor, marketing, and overall operations are low-hanging fruit," highlighted analyst David Katz.

Katz also said that the valuation of Six Flags Entertainment (FUN) is easy on the eyes. He said the company's true earnings power and cash generation potential is not currently reflected in the share price.

Jefferies assigned a price target of $59 to SIX based on multiples of 9.0X, 20.0X, and 10.0X on FY25-26 EBITDA, EPS, and free cash flow.

Shares of Six Flags (FUN) edged 0.30% higher in premarket trading on Monday. The theme park stock has traded flat over the last six weeks. Short interest stands at 9.62% of the total float.

data.title: Nvidia slips as China opens probe on anti-monopoly concerns: report
data.description:
• Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) shares slipped 2% in premarket trading on Monday as China's antitrust agency opened a probe into the Jensen-Huang led company on concerns it may have beached monopoly laws.
  • The State Administration for Market Regulation has opened the probe, according to China Central Television. The news was first reported domestically by Bloomberg.
  • Nvidia has tweaked its existing artificial intelligence accelerators for sale in China to comply with existing U.S. export control laws. Nvidia said on its most recent earnings call that it expects China to remain a "very competitive" environment, notably in the data center market.
  • Nvidia did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: Microsoft-G42 deal sees US clear advanced AI chips for export to UAE - report
data.description:
The U.S. government approved the export of advanced AI chips to a Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT)-operated facility in the United Arab Emirates, as part of the company's collaboration with G42, Axios reported on Saturday, citing two people with knowledge of the deal.

In April, it was reported that Microsoft would invest about $1.5B in the Abu Dhabi-based AI firm, giving the U.S. tech giant a minority stake and a board seat. Under the partnership, G42 would use Microsoft's cloud services to run its AI applications. In September, the two companies said they would set up two centers in Abu Dhabi to advance responsible AI goals.

However, the deal was scrutinized after U.S. lawmakers raised concerns that G42 could potentially transfer U.S. AI technology to China. They requested for a U.S. review of G42's ties to the Chinese Communist Party, military and government before the Microsoft deal advances, as per a report from Reuters.

The U.S. Commerce Department, Microsoft and G42 did not respond to a query from Seeking Alpha as of the time of publication of this report.

The approved export license requires Microsoft to prevent access to the facility by personnel who are from nations under U.S. arms embargoes or who are on the Bureau of Industry and Security's, or BIS, Entity List, the Axios report added.

The curbs include people physically in China, the Chinese government or personnel working for any organization headquartered in China, the report noted.

People sanctioned by the Treasury Department are also not allowed access, according to the report.

Earlier this year, G42 had said it was working with U.S. partners and the UAE government to adhere to AI development and deployment standards, amid concerns about its relationship with China, the Reuters' report noted.

Abu Dhabi sovereign wealth fund Mubadala Investment Company, UAE's ruling family and U.S. private equity company Silver Lake hold stakes in G42. The AI company's chairman, Sheikh Tahnoon bin Zayed Al Nahyan, is the UAE's national security advisor, the report noted.
